sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Tuya Inc. Announces Board Meeting and Quarterly Earnings Call for Q1 2026
feed.summary: Tuya Inc. has announced that its Board of Directors will meet on May 11, 2026, to approve the unaudited quarterly results for Q1 2026. The company will release these results and host an earnings call on May 12, 2026 (Hong Kong Time) to discuss its financial performance and provide insights. This event is considered price-sensitive, and shareholders are encouraged to monitor disclosures and participate in the earnings call.

feed.title: Earnings To Watch: Humana (HUM) Reports Q1 Results Tomorrow
feed.summary: Humana (NYSE:HUM) is set to report its Q1 earnings tomorrow, with expectations for 22.9% year-on-year revenue growth. The company reported a significant miss on full-year EPS guidance last quarter despite beating revenue expectations. Investors are showing positive sentiment in the health insurance sector, and Humana's stock performance ahead of earnings reflects this.

feed.title: Everest Group (EG) Q1 Earnings Report Preview: What To Look For
feed.summary: Everest Group (EG) is set to report its Q1 earnings, with expectations for flat year-on-year revenue growth after missing analyst estimates last quarter. The company's peers in the insurance segment, Stewart Information Services and First American Financial, have reported strong Q1 results, suggesting potential positive trends for the sector. Everest Group's stock is up 6.4% over the last month and heading into earnings with an average analyst price target higher than its current share price.

feed.title: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) To Report Earnings Tomorrow: Here Is What To Expect
feed.summary: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) is scheduled to report earnings after the bell on Wednesday. The market anticipates flat year-on-year revenue, an improvement from an 8.3% decrease in the same quarter last year. Competitors FedEx and Heartland Express have already reported varied Q1 results, with positive investor sentiment in the transportation and logistics sector pushing CHRW's share price up by 16.9% recently.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Citigroup (C) After A 94% One Year Share Price Surge?
feed.summary: Despite a 94% share price surge over the last year, Citigroup (C) appears undervalued according to two valuation models. The Excess Returns model suggests it's 30.3% undervalued with an intrinsic value of US$185.34 per share, while the P/E ratio, though higher than industry averages, is below Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio of 21.44x. The article further explores bullish and bearish narratives, with fair value estimates ranging from US$112.86 to US$233.04 depending on underlying assumptions.

feed.title: Pfizer (PFE) Valuation Check After New Oncology Data And Priority Review For Cancer Regimen
feed.summary: Pfizer (PFE) is under valuation scrutiny after new oncology data and FDA Priority Review for its bladder cancer regimen. Despite recent positive momentum with a 6.39% year-to-date share price return, it trails in longer-term performance. The stock is currently trading at US$26.79, appearing undervalued according to some analysts with a fair value target of $30.62, but carries risks like high debt and potential dividend cuts.

feed.title: AbbVie (ABBV) To Report Earnings Tomorrow: Here Is What To Expect
feed.summary: AbbVie (ABBV) is set to announce its earnings tomorrow before market hours. The market anticipates a 10.5% year-on-year revenue growth, an increase from the previous year's 8.4%. Analysts have shown increased bullishness on revenue estimates over the last 30 days, reflecting AbbVie's history of exceeding expectations, despite its stock being down 7% over the last month while the biotechnology sector is up 11% on average.

feed.title: Is RTX (RTX) Still Fairly Priced After Recent Pullback In Aerospace And Defense Sentiment
authors: Simply Wall St Reviewed by Bailey Pemberton
feed.summary: RTX (NYSE:RTX) has experienced a recent pullback, declining 11.4% over the last 7 days, despite a strong 41.3% return over the past year. According to Simply Wall St's Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis, RTX is currently estimated to be about 2.0% undervalued, with an intrinsic value of $176.97 per share compared to its current price of US$173.38. The company's P/E ratio of 32.18x is also below the industry average, suggesting it may be undervalued based on earnings multiples.

feed.title: KLA Corporation (KLAC) Q1 Earnings: What To Expect
feed.summary: KLA Corporation is set to report its Q1 earnings this Wednesday afternoon, with market expectations pointing to a 10.2% year-on-year revenue growth. The company has a history of exceeding Wall Street's expectations, and analysts have largely reconfirmed their estimates. Peers in the semiconductor segment, like Lam Research and Amkor, have already reported strong Q1 results, suggesting a positive outlook for KLA.

feed.title: Textron Aviation expands High‑Speed Cabin connectivity with Gogo 5G upgrade for Cessna Citation Business Jets
feed.summary: Textron Aviation announced the availability of Gogo 5G connectivity upgrades for several Cessna Citation business jets, offering faster speeds and improved WiFi. This aftermarket solution is available for various Citation models through Textron Aviation Service Centers, with plans to expand to more models soon. The upgrade aims to provide essential high-speed connectivity for business and passenger needs in flight.

feed.title: Yum! Brands (YUM) To Report Earnings Tomorrow: Here Is What To Expect
feed.summary: Yum! Brands (YUM) is set to report its earnings tomorrow, with expectations for revenue growth of 14.5% year-on-year. Analysts have grown increasingly bearish, with majority downward revisions of revenue estimates over the last 30 days. The company's peers in the restaurant segment, Domino's and Kura Sushi, have already reported mixed Q1 results.

feed.title: Earnings To Watch: Lennox (LII) Reports Q1 Results Tomorrow
feed.summary: Lennox International (LII) is scheduled to report its Q1 earnings, with analysts expecting flat year-on-year revenue growth. The company missed revenue and adjusted operating income expectations last quarter. Investor sentiment in the building products segment has been positive, with Lennox's stock up 13% over the last month.

feed.title: GE HealthCare (GEHC) Q1 Earnings Report Preview: What To Look For
feed.summary: GE HealthCare (GEHC) will report its Q1 earnings this Wednesday, with analysts expecting a 5.2% year-on-year revenue growth. The company previously beat revenue expectations and full-year EPS guidance last quarter. Investor sentiment in the healthcare equipment and supplies segment has been positive, with GE HealthCare's stock up 2.4% over the last month.

feed.title: [6-K] TFI International Inc. Current Report (Foreign Issuer) | TFII SEC Filing - Form 6-K
feed.summary: TFI International Inc. held its annual shareholder meeting on April 27, 2026, where all nine nominated directors were elected with strong majority support. Shareholders also approved the appointment of Deloitte LLP as auditor and an advisory "say on pay" resolution for executive compensation, indicating broad backing for the company’s governance and pay practices. The company filed this Form 6-K to report these voting results to the SEC.

feed.title: BCS.BVA Bond Analysis — Key Metrics
feed.summary: This article provides key metrics and an overview of the Barclays Bank PLC 4.5% 20-JAN-2027 bond (BCS.BVA FINRA). It details the issuer, issue date, maturity date, outstanding amount, face value, minimum denomination, and a fixed coupon of 4.50%. The bond overview also includes a brief description of Barclays Bank Plc's services and its founding information.

feed.title: Verisk (VRSK) Q1 Earnings Report Preview: What To Look For
feed.summary: Verisk (VRSK) is set to report its Q1 earnings, with analysts expecting a 2.6% year-on-year revenue growth, a slowdown from the previous year. The company has a history of missing Wall Street's revenue estimates, and its stock is currently down 5.3% over the last month, contrasting with a positive sentiment in the broader professional services segment. Investors will be watching how Verisk performs given its mixed past results and declining share price relative to peers.

feed.title: ADP Earnings: What To Look For From ADP
feed.summary: Automatic Data Processing (ADP) is set to announce its earnings, with analysts anticipating a 5.4% year-on-year revenue growth. The company has a history of surpassing Wall Street expectations, and despite some peers experiencing share price drops after their Q1 results, professional services stocks have seen positive sentiment. Investors will be keen to see if ADP continues its trend of strong performance against analyst targets.

feed.title: Stellantis employees raise health concerns after 5-day office return
feed.summary: Stellantis employees at the Auburn Hills headquarters have reported various health issues, including nosebleeds, migraines, and skin problems, after being required to return to the office five days a week. Michigan health and safety regulators conducted an inspection earlier this year following these reports. The concerns arose after the company mandated a full return to in-person work.

feed.title: Seritage Growth Properties (NYSE: SRG) outlines 2026 proxy and Plan of Sale
feed.summary: Seritage Growth Properties (NYSE: SRG) has announced its virtual 2026 annual shareholder meeting for June 9, 2026. Shareholders will vote on electing six trustees, ratifying Deloitte & Touche LLP as the auditor, and approving an advisory resolution on executive compensation. The proxy statement also details the company's ongoing Plan of Sale, where the board continues to explore strategic alternatives like asset sales or a company sale, and outlines the cash-based, retention-focused compensation programs for senior executives.

feed.title: BlackRock (SPRY) files amendment showing 4.98% ownership
feed.summary: BlackRock, Inc. has filed an amendment to its Schedule 13G, disclosing beneficial ownership of 4,946,412 shares of ARS Pharmaceuticals, Inc. common stock, which represents 4.98% of the class. The filing indicates BlackRock holds sole voting power for 4,860,113 shares and sole dispositive power for all 4,946,412 shares. This amendment, signed by Spencer Fleming, Managing Director, on April 27, 2026, confirms that BlackRock's ownership is less than 5% and is held for passive investment purposes.

feed.title: UGP or MPLX: Which is the better value stock right now?
feed.summary: The article "UGP or MPLX: Which is the better value stock right now?" suggests a comparison between UGP and MPLX to determine which company currently offers better value to investors, though the content itself is missing. The title indicates it's a financial analysis aimed at identifying a superior investment based on valuation metrics.

feed.title: Solid Biosciences Inc. sponsors SADS Foundation symposium to back genetic heart rhythm research
feed.summary: Solid Biosciences Inc. sponsored the SADS Foundation's Challenging Cases in Inherited Arrhythmias Symposium, demonstrating its commitment to genetic heart rhythm research. This participation aligns with the company's broader focus on advancing precision gene therapy and initiating human trials for conditions like Friedreich ataxia. Solid Biosciences continues to emphasize genetic approaches for treating inherited disorders.

feed.title: BlackRock (SMC) reports 612,798 shares (4.9%) of Summit Midstream
feed.summary: BlackRock, Inc. has filed an Amendment No. 1 to a Schedule 13G/A, disclosing a beneficial ownership of 612,798 shares, or 4.9%, of Summit Midstream Corp common stock. The filing, signed on April 27, 2026, attributes these holdings to specific BlackRock Reporting Business Units and indicates sole voting power for 602,656 shares and sole dispositive power for all 612,798 shares. This passive investment falls below the 5% threshold that would require a Schedule 13D filing for active intent.

feed.title: Did Gene Therapy Milestones and Dupixent Expansion Just Shift Regeneron Pharmaceuticals' (REGN) Investment Narrative?
feed.summary: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals recently achieved significant milestones, including FDA accelerated approval for Otarmeni, expanded pediatric approval for Dupixent, and positive Phase 3 data for cemdisiran. These advancements broaden Regeneron's portfolio into gene therapy, pediatric immunology, and complement-mediated diseases. While Dupixent's expanded approval is seen as a key growth driver, investors are also considering how new assets like cemdisiran could diversify earnings, alongside concerns about global pricing pressure on existing blockbuster drugs.

feed.title: Will Coca-Cola’s (KO) HR Shift and Sustainability Push Reshape Its Pricing Power Narrative?
feed.summary: Coca-Cola announced a new global chief people officer and is highlighting its sustainability efforts at Earthx2026. This comes as the company approaches its Q1 2026 earnings report, where investors will closely watch its pricing power and ability to balance price and volume amidst consumer trends and regulatory pressures. The article emphasizes that while these new developments are significant, the core investment narrative for Coca-Cola still revolves around brand strength, distribution, and pricing discipline.

feed.title: After a retirement, Sallie Mae gives two top executives broader roles
feed.summary: Sallie Mae (Nasdaq: SLM) has appointed Peter Graham, CFO, and Kerri Palmer, COO, as Co-Presidents, reporting to CEO Jon Witter. These appointments follow the recent retirement of the company's Chief Commercial Officer, granting Graham and Palmer broader strategic roles, with Graham overseeing strategic partnerships and Palmer leading the core private education loan business. This move aims to strengthen leadership and execution of the company's growth strategy.

feed.title: Tennessee Valley Authority board names interim president and CEO
feed.summary: The Tennessee Valley Authority board has named an interim president and CEO. This announcement follows the current president and CEO, Don Moul, revealing his plans to retire effective July 1st.

feed.title: SCYNEXIS (NASDAQ: SCYX) seeks approval for reverse stock split
feed.summary: SCYNEXIS (NASDAQ: SCYX) is seeking stockholder approval for a reverse stock split at a ratio between 1-for-5 and 1-for-10 to maintain its listing on The Nasdaq Capital Market. The virtual special meeting is scheduled for May 19, 2026, where shareholders will vote on the proposal to increase the per-share price and avoid potential delisting proceedings by June 15, 2026. If approved, the board will have the discretion to determine the exact ratio and implement the split, which will proportionally reduce outstanding shares and adjust equity incentive plans.

feed.title: Solaris Energy Infrastructure Announces First Quarter 2026 Results, Updated Earnings Guidance, Signing Of Third Long-term Contract With Global Technology Company
feed.summary: Solaris Energy Infrastructure (NYSE:SEI) announced strong first quarter 2026 results, exceeding expectations with revenue of $196 million and Adjusted EBITDA of $84 million. The company significantly increased its second quarter 2026 Adjusted EBITDA guidance and secured a third long-term power contract with a global technology company for over 600 MW of capacity. Solaris also expanded its generation capacity to 3,100 MW through recent transactions and approved a $0.12 per share dividend.

feed.title: RFAIR Price History for RF Acquisition Corp II Right Stock
feed.summary: This article provides an in-depth look at the price history of RF Acquisition Corp II Right (RFAIR) stock, including daily prices, trade data, and corporate actions. It details 52-week key points, new highs, and new lows, offering investors tools for historical data analysis and performance tracking. The content is provided by Barchart.com, which also offers various membership levels for enhanced data access.

feed.title: BlackRock holds 1.69M shares in Spire Global (SPIR) per Schedule 13G
feed.summary: BlackRock, Inc. has filed a Schedule 13G, disclosing beneficial ownership of 1,694,667 shares of Spire Global, Inc. Class A stock, which accounts for 5.1% of the company's class. The filing, dated March 31, 2026, indicates that BlackRock holds sole voting power over 1,666,602 shares and sole dispositive power over all 1,694,667 shares. This disclosure signifies BlackRock's passive investment exceeding the 5% threshold, requiring public reporting.

feed.title: Manhattan Associates and Genuine Parts Company Go Live with Next-Generation Warehouse Management System in Brisbane
feed.summary: Manhattan Associates and Genuine Parts Company (GPC) have successfully implemented Manhattan Active® Warehouse Management at GPC's Brisbane distribution center. This go-live project modernizes GPC's DC operations by replacing legacy systems with a unified, cloud-native platform, enhancing visibility, efficiency, and scalability across its supply chain. The new system, including Honeywell Voice for Manhattan Active, is expected to improve inventory accuracy, labor productivity, and fulfillment processes, forming a stronger foundation for GPC's future growth and operational performance.

feed.title: Best Dividend Stocks for $1,000 Investment in 2026 | Realty Income, ExxonMobil, JPMorgan ETF - News and Statistics
feed.summary: This article identifies three top dividend stocks for a $1,000 investment in 2026, focusing on their potential for steady income. The recommended options are Realty Income for its consistent monthly dividends, ExxonMobil for its strong performance and quarterly income, and the JPMorgan Nasdaq Equity Premium Income ETF which uses a covered call strategy to generate monthly dividends. Each selection highlights reliability and income generation as key benefits for investors.

feed.title: Fincantieri Launches "Norwegian Aura" at Monfalcone Shipyard
feed.summary: Fincantieri has launched the large cruise ship “Norwegian Aura” at its Monfalcone shipyard for Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCL). The ship, which is 10% larger than previous Prima Plus Class vessels, is expected to be delivered in spring 2027 and features a large water park and 1,976 cabins. This launch reinforces the strategic partnership between Fincantieri and NCLH, which includes orders for three additional cruise ships scheduled for delivery through 2037.

feed.title: VITL SHAREHOLDER ALERT: Bronstein, Gewirtz and Grossman, LLC Announces that Vital Farms, Inc. Shareholders Have Opportunity to Lead Class Action Lawsuit!
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC has announced a class action lawsuit against Vital Farms, Inc. (NASDAQ:VITL) and its officers. The lawsuit alleges that Vital Farms made false and misleading statements regarding delays in its new ERP system rollout, which caused the company to miss its full-year 2025 earnings guidance. Investors who purchased Vital Farms securities between May 8, 2025, and February 26, 2026, have until May 26, 2026, to request to be appointed as lead plaintiff.

feed.title: Is Robert Half (RHI) One of the Best Dividend Stocks Yielding at Least 7% According to Hedge Funds?
feed.summary: William Blair recently upgraded Robert Half Inc. (RHI) to Outperform, citing compelling risk/reward and early signs of improving sentiment among investors. The firm estimates about 40% upside for RHI in a base-case scenario over the next 12 months, with even greater potential in a stronger cyclical rebound. Robert Half's management provided Q1 2026 revenue guidance between $1.275 billion and $1.375 billion, and EPS between $0.20 and $0.30.

feed.title: How to fix cybersecurity's agentic AI identity crisis
feed.summary: The increasing adoption of autonomous AI agents in enterprises presents significant security challenges due to their ability to execute commands and make decisions independently. Traditional security models struggle with agent identity and authentication, creating vulnerabilities exemplified by OpenClaw. To mitigate these risks, enterprises must implement robust security frameworks focusing on agent identity, authorization, isolation, prompt injection defenses, and comprehensive monitoring and incident response.

feed.title: Texas Instruments Earnings Rise on Data Center and Industrial Demand
feed.summary: Texas Instruments reported strong first-quarter 2026 earnings, with revenue reaching $4.8 billion, a 19% increase year-over-year, largely driven by data center and industrial demand. The company expects second-quarter revenue between $5 billion and $5.4 billion and plans continued investments in manufacturing, technology, and strategic acquisitions like Silicon Labs to sustain long-term growth and capitalize on the semiconductor market recovery.

feed.title: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) stock falls amid market uptick: What investors need to know
feed.summary: This article analyzes why Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) stock dipped despite a broader market increase, highlighting its recent price movements and comparing them against industry trends and competitor performance. Investors should note the stock's underperformance relative to the S&P 500 and the challenges faced by its sector.

feed.title: Smart Money is Buying Quest Diagnostics Inc. (DGX)
feed.summary: Institutional investors are showing strong buying interest in Quest Diagnostics Inc. (DGX), with notable increases in holdings by value-focused and quantitative managers. This accumulation reflects confidence in Quest's robust diagnostics business, stable cash flow, and potential to benefit from continued demand for testing and population health trends. The article highlights key investors like Chris Davis, Jeremy Grantham, and Joel Greenblatt increasing their stakes, suggesting DGX is seen as an attractive defensive healthcare stock.

feed.title: PLLD Bond Price and Chart — SWB:PLLD
feed.summary: This article provides details on the Prudential Financial, Inc. 4.5% 15-SEP-2047 bond (SWB:PLLD) traded on the Stuttgart Stock Exchange. It includes key terms such as outstanding amount, face value, coupon, maturity date, and term to maturity. The article also offers a brief overview of Prudential Financial, Inc., outlining its segments and business operations.

feed.title: ROSEN, LEADING INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages SES AI Corporation Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action First Filed by the Firm – SES
feed.summary: The Rosen Law Firm has filed a class action lawsuit against SES AI Corporation (NYSE: SES) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between January 29, 2025, and March 4, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that SES AI made false and misleading statements regarding its business prospects, revenue generation, and financial performance. Investors who purchased SES AI securities during this period are encouraged to secure counsel, with a deadline to move the Court as lead plaintiff by June 26, 2026.

feed.title: Sun Life HK's new business surges
feed.summary: Sun Life Hong Kong achieved a record-breaking HK$11.8 billion in new business annualized premium equivalent (APE) in 2025, marking a 46% year-over-year increase. This strong performance, driven by growth across all distribution channels, positions the company among the top five nonbank insurers in Hong Kong. The company plans to capitalize on this momentum by focusing on high-net-worth clients, enhancing digital capabilities, and leveraging AI for personalized solutions.

feed.title: Executive advisor at RGA (NYSE: RGA) receives new equity awards
feed.summary: Simon Wainwright, Executive Advisor to the CEO at Reinsurance Group of America (NYSE: RGA), has been granted new equity awards. These include stock appreciation rights for 1,609 shares at an exercise price of $200.50 and 557 restricted share units, both settling in common stock. The awards will vest in annual installments over three years, fully vesting by March 19, 2029, aligning Wainwright's compensation with the company's long-term stock performance.

feed.title: CFO at Reinsurance Group (NYSE: RGA) receives new SAR and RSU grants
feed.summary: Andre Axel, the EVP and Chief Financial Officer of Reinsurance Group of America (NYSE: RGA), was granted equity-based compensation awards on March 19, 2026. These awards include 8,642 Stock Appreciation Rights with an exercise price of $200.50 per share and 2,993 restricted share units. Both types of awards will settle in common stock and vest in annual increments of 33 1/3% over three years, with full vesting by March 19, 2029.

feed.title: Reinsurance Group of America (NYSE: RGA) grants SARs and RSUs to legal chief
feed.summary: Reinsurance Group of America (NYSE: RGA) has granted equity-based awards to its Executive Vice President and Chief Legal Officer, To My Chi. These awards include 3,457 stock appreciation rights with a conversion price of $200.50 per share and 1,197 restricted share units. Both the SARs and RSUs will settle in common stock and vest in 33 1/3% increments annually, fully vesting by March 19, 2029, as compensation for service.

feed.title: RGA (NYSE: RGA) grants SARs, RSUs to EVP Jonathan Porter
feed.summary: Jonathan Porter, EVP and Global Chief Risk Officer at Reinsurance Group of America (NYSE: RGA), has been granted 3,176 stock appreciation rights (SARs) with an exercise price of $200.50 and 1,100 restricted share units (RSUs). These equity-based compensation awards will settle in common stock and vest in equal 33 1/3% installments annually, fully vesting by March 19, 2029. This report is a compensation award, not an open-market transaction.

feed.title: RGA (NYSE: RGA) EVP Cormac Galvin receives stock appreciation rights and RSUs
feed.summary: Reinsurance Group of America (RGA) EVP Cormac Galvin was granted stock appreciation rights for 1,572 shares with an exercise price of $200.50 and 544 restricted share units. These equity-based awards, granted on March 19, 2026, will vest in equal one-third installments annually, fully vesting by March 19, 2029, and settle in common stock. The filing, which was late due to an administrative error, shows these awards are part of his compensation and not open-market transactions.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean doubles down on its biggest cruise ships yet with two new orders
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group has ordered two more Icon Class ships, named Icon 6 and Icon 7, to be delivered in 2029 and 2030, demonstrating their confidence in the mega-ship formula. These new orders signify a major expansion in their fleet, with the Icon Class ships proving highly popular since the debut of Icon of the Seas in 2024. The strategy focuses on creating distinct "neighborhoods" and catering to multi-generational appeal, leading to strong demand and profitability for the company.

feed.title: T. Rowe nabs coveted JP Morgan partnership, not long after signing one with Goldman Sachs -- both trace to its 2024 hire of model portfolios executive Josh Horesh from BlackRock
feed.summary: T. Rowe Price has secured a significant partnership with JP Morgan, following a similar collaboration with Goldman Sachs. Both partnerships are attributed to the firm's strategic hire of Josh Horesh from BlackRock in 2024, a known expert in model portfolios. This indicates T. Rowe Price's aggressive expansion in the model portfolio space through key talent acquisition.
